author: obsidian-ttrpg-community
description: This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff. Random generator engine. Render `randomness` codeblocks inline with rich text output, plus support for legacy Inspiration Pad Pro 3 .ipt files.

manifest.author: Josh
manifest.description: Random table generator for TTRPG and creative writing. Compatible with the Inspiration Pad Pro 3 (IPP3) syntax. Roll inline, lock results, browse generators in a sidebar.
manifest.name: Randomness

name: Randomness

releases.author: ObsidianTTRPGProject
releases.description:
Robustness release for file discovery and Use: resolution (rolls up 1.0.3–1.0.10).

Fixed: files added outside Obsidian now resolve (index + resolver scan the disk, not just Obsidian's metadata); fixed a root-path bug that made that scan find nothing.
Fixed: inline and codeblock Use: now use the same file index as the rest of the plugin.
Fixed: legacy Windows-style sub-path references (Use: nbos\names\orc.ipt) resolve against differently-organised vaults.
Fixed: macOS NFD/NFC Unicode filename matching.
Added: "Diagnose generator resolution" command — dumps the raw on-disk folder listing vs. what's indexed, exposing mismatches (hidden extensions, trailing spaces, etc.).
Added: index-rebuild now reports file/table counts; clearer "not found" errors.

The original report turned out to be a file named portraits.ipt.md with the .md hidden by the OS — the new diagnostic command is built to catch exactly that.
1,018 tests passing.

Massive thanks to Hannu for raising the issue and assisting with the testing.
releases.name: 1.0.10 - Robustness release for file discovery and Use: resolution (rolls up 1.0.3–1.0.10).

releases.author: ObsidianTTRPGProject
releases.description:
Randomness v1.0.2
Bug-fix release.

Fixed: Use: targets failing to resolve for .ipt files added via Finder/Explorer — file lookups now fall back to a direct directory scan that catches files Obsidian hasn't indexed yet (case-insensitively). Reported on macOS.
Fixed: the "not found" error now points at the likely cause (an unindexed file) and suggests reloading or rebuilding the index, rather than implying a wrong path.

If a Use: still won't resolve after updating, reload Obsidian or run Rebuild generator index.
994 tests passing.
releases.name: 1.0.2 - A bug-fix release addressing Use: file resolution for generators added outside Obsidian.

releases.author: ObsidianTTRPGProject
releases.description:
# Town Forge 1.0.1

Adds the settlement **size** to exported place notes, so templates and Randomness rolls can scale their content to the settlement.

## What's new
- **Settlement size in frontmatter** — each exported place note now carries a `size:` property set to the settlement's size (one of `hamlet`, `village`, `small_town`, `town`, `large_town`, `small_city`, `city`, `large_city`, `metropolis`).
- **`{{size}}` template token** — use it in your place templates; a Templater body can read `fm.size` and pass it to a size-aware table (e.g. banded shop stock).

## Notes
- Place notes are written during a TTRPG Tools: Maps export (full-mode maps).
- See [docs/randomness-templater-setup.md](docs/randomness-templater-setup.md) for wiring `size` (and `subtype`) into Randomness + Templater.

## Install
Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `versions.json` below into `.obsidian/plugins/town-forge/`, then enable under Settings → Community Plugins.
releases.name: 1.0.1 - Adds the settlement **size** to exported place notes

releases.author: ObsidianTTRPGProject
releases.description:
**Randomness v1.0.0 — First stable release**
Full implementation and documentation of the public JavaScript API, plus a complete PF2e settlement generator library. The plugin is now stable for general use.
JS API (surface version 1.0.0) — fully implemented and documented
Every method of the public API is implemented, covered by 64 dedicated tests, and documented in API.md. Other plugins and Templater scripts can roll generators via app.plugins.plugins["randomness"].api.

rollUnscoped(name, opts?) — roll a generator found anywhere in the vault, ignoring note scope. The method for note generation and automation (e.g. a Templater template building a note from a shared generator library).
Scoped roll(name, opts?) for rolling in a note's context.
promptValues — override a generator's prompts by label.
seed — deterministic, reproducible rolls.
filePath — disambiguate when two files define the same table.
Ambiguity warning: rollUnscoped logs a clear console warning naming the colliding files instead of silently picking one.
tablesWithSources() — list tables with their source files; the go-to diagnostic for "why did I get the wrong generator?"
onRoll(cb) — subscribe to every roll attempt.
"Rebuild generator index" command.

The API surface is committed: breaking changes to it will bump the major version.
PF2e settlement generator library
A drop-in generator set (pf2e-settlement-generators.zip) covering a whole town, all TF- namespaced and driveable from the API with town / shopType / shopName prompts:

Shops: general, weapon, armor, alchemy, magic. TF-ShopPick (emits subtype|name) and TF-ShopByType for coherent name↔type note generation.
Locations: inn, tavern, stable, market, temple, castle, manor, wizard's tower, undertaker, barracks, dock, mill, farm.
Guilds: six categories (craft, religious, military, criminal, arcane, civic), each spanning a good→murky→dark slant. TF-GuildPick / TF-GuildByType mirror the shop pattern.
Standalone names for note titles: TF-CastleName, TF-InnName, TF-BarracksName, TF-ThievesGuildName, … one per type.
Umbrella pickers: TF-Location (anything) / TF-Place (non-shop).

**Also in this release**

CSS lint: the broken-link style uses a dashed border-bottom instead of an underline decoration, clearing the community-plugin linter warning. Identical visual result.

**Install**
Manual: download main.js, manifest.json, and styles.css (or randomness-1.0.0.zip) into <vault>/.obsidian/plugins/randomness/, then enable in Settings → Community plugins.
BRAT: add the repo; BRAT picks up the release assets.
Generators (optional): unzip pf2e-settlement-generators.zip into your vault, then run Rebuild generator index.
Tests
939 passing across 35 suites, including 64 dedicated API tests.

**Thanks**
🙏 [@pjjelly17](https://github.com/pjjelly17) — whose PR #1 proposed the public JS API that this release implements and documents.
releases.name: 1.0.0 - first stable release

releases.author: ObsidianTTRPGProject
releases.description:
**Randomness v0.7.0 — Full JS API (implementation + documentation) + PF2e settlement generator library**
This release delivers the complete public JavaScript API — fully implemented, tested, and documented — and ships an expanded PF2e settlement generator set.
JS API (surface version 1.0.0) — fully implemented and documented

Complete public API via app.plugins.plugins["randomness"].api, backed by 64 dedicated tests and documented in a new API.md.
roll(name, opts?) — scoped roll (sees a note's Use: graph and same-note tables).
rollUnscoped(name, opts?) — rolls a generator found anywhere in the vault, ignoring note scope; the method for note generation and automation.
rollExpression(expr, opts?), tables(), tablesWithSources(), onRoll(), and version.
promptValues (override prompts by label), seed (deterministic rolls), and filePath (disambiguate name collisions) wired through to the engine.
Ambiguity warning: rollUnscoped logs a clear console warning naming colliding files instead of silently picking one.
Added "Rebuild generator index" command.
README gains a "Scripting: the JS API" section and a Features bullet, both linking to API.md.

**PF2e settlement generators (TF- namespaced, town/name-aware)**

Shops (general/weapon/armor/alchemy/magic), plus TF-ShopPick/TF-ShopByType for coherent name↔type note generation.
Locations: inn, tavern, stable, market, temple, castle, manor, wizard's tower, undertaker, barracks, dock, mill, farm.
Guilds across six categories (craft/religious/military/criminal/arcane/civic), each spanning a good→murky→dark slant, with TF-GuildPick/TF-GuildByType.
Standalone name tables (TF-CastleName, TF-BarracksName, TF-ThievesGuildName, …) and umbrella pickers TF-Location/TF-Place.

**Housekeeping**

Bumped manifest.json, package.json, and versions.json to 0.7.0.
Added CHANGELOG.md.
939 tests passing across 35 suites (64 API-specific).

**Thanks**

🙏 [@pjjelly17](https://github.com/pjjelly17) — whose PR #1 proposed the public JS API that this release implements and documents.
releases.name: 0.7.0 - JS API + PF2e settlement generator library

releases.author: ObsidianTTRPGProject
releases.description:
Randomness v0.4.4 — Initial release
An Obsidian plugin for running random generators directly in your notes. Compatible with the Inspiration Pad Pro 3 syntax (NBOS), with extensions for Obsidian wiki-links and inline interaction.
Highlights

Roll generators from ```randomness codeblocks or inline with `rdm:[@TableName]`
Lock results into your note (🔒) or reroll (🎲) on demand
Right-sidebar browser pane showing every generator in your vault, with pinnable favourites
Inline images and links via Obsidian wiki-syntax: ![[portrait.png]] and [[Note]] render natively inside roll results
Custom view for .ipt files — click one in the file explorer to roll it
Table-name autocomplete: type  `rdm:[@ and pick from every generator in your vault; out-of-scope picks auto-add the Use: import
In-app reference covering the full syntax (Settings → Open reference, or command palette)
Full IPP3 syntax support: dice, variables, filters, conditionals, repetitions, prompts, lookups, decks

Installation
Drop main.js, manifest.json, and styles.css into .obsidian/plugins/randomness/ in your vault, then enable the plugin in Settings → Community plugins. Or use BRAT pointing at this repo.
Compatibility
Requires Obsidian 1.4.0 or later. Works on desktop and mobile. MIT-licensed code; uses the Paizo Community Use Policy for any bundled Pathfinder rules text.
releases.name: 0.4.4 - First Public Release
